pack_name: Law and Legal Sector Compliance Overlay
description: Sector-specific screening controls for UK solicitors, law firms, legal-service providers, client-care documentation, AML controls, client money, confidentiality, and legal professional conduct.

metadata.scope: UK sector overlay controls for Law and Legal Sector Compliance Overlay. Intended to be used with uk_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official UK legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.
metadata.territorial_scope_note: UK law can differ across England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. This starter pack uses UK-wide sources where available and flags devolved or nation-specific application for human review.

rules.title: SRA authorisation or regulated legal-service evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for SRA authorisation, regulated law firm status, SRA ID, authorised body, or practising certificate evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Client confidentiality and privilege controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for client confidentiality, legal professional privilege, confidential information, or client-file protection evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: AML risk assessment and CDD controls are documented where in scope
rules.summary: Checks for firm-wide AML risk assessment, matter-level risk assessment, CDD, source of funds, beneficial ownership, or MLRO evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Client money and client-account controls are documented where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for client money, client account, separate account, withdrawals, reconciliation, or billing-before-transfer evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Costs, complaints, or transparency information is present where public-facing
rules.summary: Checks for costs information, complaints handling, service information, transparency rules, or client-care information.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
